name: St. Gallen Monastery Tart (St. Gallen)
recipeInstructions:
(Units: 100 g = 1/4 cup; 1 dl = 1/2 cup; 180°C = 350°F; 200°C = 400°F; 230°C = 450°F; 250°C = 500°F; 2.5 cm = 1 inch)
The Story:
It was an Irish monk who established the monastery of St. Gallen in AD 613
and the town developed around it. The ruling of the monastery had a great
influence over the lives of the surrounding community, which did not gain
its independence until the 14th Century. Although the townsfolk and
countryfolk sometimes suffered under their monastic rulers, they also
inherited certain advantages, one of which is shown in this recipe. This
proves that it is not just a legend that monks enjoyed good food.
The recipe:
Cream the butter and sugar until smooth.
Mix the almonds, cocoa, cinnamon, flour, and baking powder together in a separate bowl.
Add the milk, beaten egg white, and dry ingredient mixture to the creamed butter mixture.
Combine quickly until a soft dough forms, leaving the sides of the bowl clean.
Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Line a buttered flan ring or tin with about three-quarters of the dough. Spread jam over the bottom.
Use the remaining dough to create strips, then criss-cross lattice fashion over the tart. Brush with beaten egg yolk.
Bake at 180°C for 45 minutes.
Serve cold.
